item.title: No, I Won't Download Your App. The Web Version is A-OK.
item.description: As someone who prefers using services via their websites, I’ve gotten terribly jaded lately. Almost everyone wants me, and by extension, you, to use their darn apps to consume content and off their web versions.

item.title: Surveillance Findings: Age Verification as Mass Surveillance Infrastructure
item.description: OSINT investigation documenting how age verification laws across three countries funnel citizen biometric data through a 269-check surveillance pipeline with government reporting capabilities.

item.title: AI Did It in 12 Minutes. It Took Me 10 Hours to Fix It
item.description: I've been working on personal projects since the 2000s. One thing I've always been adamant about is understanding the code I write. Even when Stack Overflow came along, I was that annoying guy who tol

item.title: Advanced Search for YouTube - Playlists.at
item.description: Advanced search for YouTube videos with filters for duration, date, title, and more.

item.title: GitHub - arman-bd/guppylm: A ~9M parameter LLM that talks like a small fish.
item.description: A ~9M parameter LLM that talks like a small fish. Contribute to arman-bd/guppylm development by creating an account on GitHub.

item.title: Microsoft Hasn’t Had a Coherent GUI Strategy Since Petzold
item.description: A few years ago I was in a meeting with developers and someone asked a simple question: “What’s the right framework for a new Windows desktop app?” Dead silence. One person sugges…

item.title: The Free Market Lie: Why Switzerland Has 25 Gbit Internet and America Doesn't
item.description: The Free Market Lie: Why Switzerland Has 25 Gbit Internet and America Doesn't

item.title: ABS (Anti-Lock Brakes) - How They Work and Why You Care!
item.description: From the history of early ABS systems to the latest complex vehicle stability controls, Dave takes you on a journey from 1970 to the present, covering how and why ABS brakes work.
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Leaving Middle East Till Things Cool
item.description: In early March, due to the unexpected war in the ME, I was contemplating my next move. Should I stay or leave? Flights were still taking off from Dubai, albeit at a limited frequency. Enterprising car rental companies were offering package deals to wealthy expats where they'd drive you to Oman and get you on a plane to wherever (Oman was more open so there were more flights to more places).
item.author: (rozumem)

item.title: Framework? I sure hope it does
item.description: the good, the bad, and the very ugly of the framework laptop

item.title: The Impossible Bracket – Josh Pearlson
item.description: The odds of a perfect March Madness bracket are 1 in 9.2 quintillion. Warren Buffett bet a billion dollars on it, and he was never worried about paying out.

item.title: Google AI Edge Gallery-app - App Store
item.description: Download Google AI Edge Gallery van Google in de App Store. Bekijk schermafbeeldingen, beoordelingen en recensies, gebruikerstips en meer apps zoals Google AI…

item.title: I give up on neovim
item.description: Note: English is not my first language. The text below was written by me and corrected by an AI. I then corrected the AI’s corrected text, so there are probably still some wording errors In 2022, I made a New Year’s resolution to switch from Chrome to Firefox, and from VS Code to Neovim. My goal was to reduce my dependence on GAFAM tools, and it turned out to be a good decision considering this.

item.title: How to Get Better at Guitar
item.description: Years ago I watched a video where guitar teacher Justin Sandercoe explained a way to get better at guitar. It has changed my playing, and it might change yours, too.

item.title: The Codification Treadmill
item.description: The person who replaces you probably won't be AI. It'll be someone from the next department over who learned to use it.

item.title: Good APIs Age Slowly
item.description: Good APIs do not win on first impression. They survive change. This post looks at why stable APIs expose less, assume less, and age better over time.
item.author: (https://www.facebook.com/yusufaytas)

item.title: Have you considered being more lucky?
item.description: Three tips on how to be more lucky.

item.title: Is All Software Converging
Fine-tuning Whisper to my speech: 27% to 6.5% WER
item.description: How I fine-tuned Whisper to recognize my atypical speech, dropping WER from 27% to 6.5% by freezing the decoder and only training the encoder.

item.title: Don't Yell at Your LLM
item.description: LLMs don't have feelings, but they have a dataset based on human interaction. It's probably not a great idea to behave in a way that produced negative results in the dataset.

item.title: The Anatomy of SMS Delivery: Routing and Carrier Flow
item.description: Most SMS APIs hide routing. This guide explains how SMS delivery really works, from API request to pricing, execution, tracking and carrier outcome
item.author: (https://blog.bridgexapi.io/@bridgexapi)

item.title: How Claude Code Builds a System Prompt
item.description: Context engineering in action.

item.title: In Defense of Comments
item.description: Comments are the most interesting part of the internet.

item.title: Domain-Driven Design: Lean Aggregates
item.description: Design aggregates around true consistency boundaries, not around all related data, to keep domain logic clear and write paths performant as the system grows.

item.title: Bazel is not scary anymore
item.description: LLMs have eliminated Bazel's biggest adoption barrier — the steep learning curve. They're fluent in Starlark, can generate BUILD files, and make getting started surprisingly easy.

item.title: The browser is eating itself
item.description: What is a web browser in a post-HTML and post-document era?

item.title: Code freezes
item.description: Code freezes can undermine the very thing they are meant to protect.

item.title: My university uses prompt injection to catch cheaters
item.description: Web developer and cybersecurity researcher

item.title: Everything I hate about the Mac
ILLEGAL 3D Rendering Techniques (N64)
item.description:
